description:
Trigger: HSM contains multiple objects with the same CKA_LABEL of the same class — either from prior crashed put()s that left duplicates, or from operator-side mistakes, or from a put() that succeeded but then destroy_object failed for one of the existing handles (the destroy loop at line 436-438 swallows individual failures with let _ =).

Bad behavior: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:139-145 calls find_objects() and takes .into_iter().next(). PKCS#11 does not specify enumeration order. After a partial put(), get() may return either the new or a stale value, with no error and no warning logged. The doc on put() (lines 364-378) acknowledges 'indeterminate read' but the read path itself silently picks one.

Fix options: (a) if find_objects returns >1 handle for DATA class on get(), return SecretError::Backend with a clear 'duplicate objects on token' message, forcing the operator to clean up; (b) sort handles by handle ID and consistently return the smallest, so reads are at least deterministic between calls until a put resolves the duplicates; (c) document the contract that callers must guarantee uniqueness. Option (a) is the safest for a security backend.
title: find_object silently picks one of N duplicate-label objects without error or warning
